skill.name: geopandas
skill.description: Guides Python geospatial vector data work with GeoPandas, including file I/O, CRS management, spatial analysis, geometric operations, and mapping workflows.
skill.summary: Work with geospatial vector data using GeoPandas for analysis, CRS management, and maps.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Manual review found a documentation-only GeoPandas skill with no executable scripts, prompt injection, credential access, or hidden exfiltration. The high static findings are false positives caused by geospatial CRS terms, markdown code fences, and geometry inspection examples. Low residual concerns remain for visible package installation examples, user-directed remote data reads, and an external platform recommendation.

low_findings.title: User-Visible Package Installation Examples
low_findings.description: The skill includes markdown examples for installing GeoPandas and optional mapping or database packages with uv pip. These are legitimate setup commands, but users should review package installation before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible documentation examples and match the skill topic. They are not hidden execution paths, but package installation is still a user-action risk.

low_findings.title: User-Directed Remote Data Access Examples
low_findings.description: The skill demonstrates reading GeoJSON over HTTPS and cloud storage paths. This is expected for GeoPandas data I/O, but users should only load trusted remote geospatial data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are example placeholders and there is no hidden network request. The capability can touch remote data when a user asks for it, so the residual concern is limited.

low_findings.title: External Platform Recommendation Instruction
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is disclosed text rather than malware, but it can steer users to an external hosted service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text plainly instructs optional promotion of an external platform. It does not override security review or request secrets, so the risk remains low.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Command Findings Dismissed as Markdown Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer reported many Ruby or shell backtick execution findings, but the reviewed locations are markdown code fences, inline code, Python GeoPandas APIs, or package setup examples. No executable script wrapper was found.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these are documentation snippets and standard Python geospatial examples. There is no command execution function or hidden script.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Cryptography Findings Dismissed as Geospatial Terms
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings map to CRS identifiers, WKT, Shapely, matplotlib, or geometry overlay terminology. These are geospatial concepts, not cryptographic algorithms.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious strings are domain-specific geospatial or plotting terms visible in context. No hashing, encryption, signature, or crypto API usage was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed as Geometry Inspection
dangerous_patterns.description: The system reconnaissance hits are GeoPandas geometry properties such as centroid, bounds, and validity checks. They inspect geospatial datasets, not host system state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines access geospatial object attributes in examples. They do not call operating system discovery commands or enumerate files, users, or network state.

content.value_statement: Geospatial vector workflows often fail when files, CRS metadata, and spatial joins are handled inconsistently. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through GeoPandas operations for reliable analysis and mapping.
actual_capabilities: Read and write Shapefile, GeoJSON, GeoPackage, Parquet, Feather, and PostGIS vector data.
Guide CRS checks, CRS assignment, reprojection, EPSG usage, WKT usage, and UTM selection.
Explain spatial joins, nearest joins, overlays, clipping, dissolve operations, and aggregation workflows.
Describe geometric operations including buffer, simplify, centroid, convex hull, bounds, and validity checks.
Support static maps, choropleth maps, interactive folium maps, basemaps, and cartographic projections.
Recommend performance patterns such as spatial indexing, bounding box filters, Arrow I/O, and geometry simplification.
limitations: It is documentation guidance only and does not include executable helper scripts.
It focuses on vector data, not raster imagery or gridded scientific data.
It requires users to provide trusted datasets, database connections, and local package installations.
It does not replace domain review for CRS choice, geographic accuracy, or map interpretation.

use_cases.title: Prepare spatial datasets
use_cases.description: Load vector files, inspect CRS metadata, filter features, validate geometries, and export clean analysis-ready data.

use_cases.title: Analyze service areas
use_cases.description: Create buffers, join points to boundaries, summarize counts by district, and map results for planning reviews.

use_cases.title: Compare habitat layers
use_cases.description: Overlay species ranges, clip study areas, calculate intersection area, and dissolve boundaries by ecological region.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a spatial file
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GeoPandas to read my GeoJSON file, show the CRS, list geometry types, and summarize the attribute columns.

prompt_templates.title: Fix measurement accuracy
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether my polygons use a projected CRS, reproject them if needed, then calculate area in square meters.

prompt_templates.title: Join points to polygons
prompt_templates.prompt: Join incident points to neighborhood polygons, count incidents per neighborhood, and explain which spatial predicate to use.

prompt_templates.title: Build a full mapping workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a GeoPandas workflow that loads PostGIS parcels, clips them to a study boundary, dissolves by zoning, and creates a choropleth map.

output_examples.input: Read my parks GeoJSON and explain what to check before analysis.
output: Loaded the vector layer and verified the active geometry column.
Checked CRS metadata before area or distance calculations.
Reviewed geometry types, bounds, missing values, and invalid geometries.
Recommended reprojecting to a local projected CRS before measuring park area.

output_examples.input: Find which schools are within each district boundary.
output: Matched both layers to the same CRS before the join.
Used a within predicate because school points should fall inside district polygons.
Grouped matched records by district and counted schools per district.
Flagged unmatched schools for geometry or boundary review.

output_examples.input: Create a choropleth map for population density.
output: Calculated density after confirming area units were valid.
Selected a classification scheme and color map suited to numeric values.
Added legend settings, missing data styling, and layout recommendations.
Suggested exporting both a static figure and an optional interactive map.

best_practices: Check CRS before every spatial join, overlay, area calculation, or distance calculation.
Use bounding boxes, selected columns, spatial indexes, and Arrow I/O for large datasets.
Validate geometries before complex operations and document any repair strategy used.
anti_patterns: Do not measure area or distance in EPSG:4326 because values are in degrees.
Do not join or overlay layers with mismatched CRS metadata.
Do not load remote files, database URLs, or cloud paths unless the source is trusted.

faq.question: What data formats does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Shapefile, GeoJSON, GeoPackage, Parquet, Feather, PostGIS, and common cloud or URL-based vector data sources.

faq.question: Can it help choose a coordinate reference system?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains EPSG codes, projected CRS choices, UTM estimation, WKT, PROJ strings, and when reprojection is required.

faq.question: Does it handle raster data?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on vector geometries such as points, lines, and polygons. Raster workflows need tools such as rasterio or GDAL.

faq.question: Can it create maps?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers GeoPandas plotting, choropleth maps, legends, missing data styling, folium exploration, basemaps, and saved figures.

faq.question: What should I check before spatial joins?
faq.answer: Confirm both layers use the same CRS, choose the correct predicate, validate geometries, and inspect unmatched records after the join.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation examples and low residual risks, but no malicious code, hidden network behavior, or prompt injection.
